commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a bounded preserved-output hook to command execution, uses it for cron command summaries, and redacts preserved cron command summaries before announce and completion webhook delivery.

PR surface: Source +221, Tests +140. Total +361 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main is source-reproducible because command output is tail-truncated before cron command summaries are stored and delivered; I did not run a live cron job in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining work needs contributor proof and a security-sensitive redaction repair before normal maintainer review; automation cannot supply the contributor's real setup proof.

target.title: fix(cron): preserve action-required command output
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96346.

Cron command jobs currently keep only the tail of captured stdout/stderr when `outputMaxBytes` is exceeded. That is usually fine for failures, but it breaks command flows where the actionable line appears near the start and the command later emits noisy output. Device-login and setup commands commonly print a URL/code first, then verbose logs; the cron run summary, run log, announce delivery, and completion webhook can then lose the only line the operator needs.

## Summary

- Adds an opt-in output preservation hook to `runCommandWithTimeout()` without changing default exec capture behavior.
- Uses that hook only for cron command jobs to preserve action-required lines such as device/login/auth code instructions even when the tail capture truncates them.
- Builds cron command summaries with an `action-required output preserved:` block before the bounded stdout/stderr tail.
- Keeps run logs and diagnostics useful for operator recovery.
- Redacts preserved action-required URLs/codes before command summaries are sent through cron announce or completion webhook delivery.
- Keeps the fix cron-scoped: ordinary exec/tool calls still use the existing tail-bound capture behavior unless they explicitly opt into preserved lines.

## Security / Delivery Boundary

The preserved action lines are intended for operator-facing cron history and diagnostics. They may include setup URLs or auth/device codes, so this PR does not blindly broadcast the unredacted text through cron delivery:

- cron run summary / diagnostics keep the actionable line for operator recovery
- cron announce delivery uses a redacted command summary
- cron completion webhook payloads use a redacted command summary and diagnostics summary
- non-command cron jobs are unchanged

## Evidence

Local focused validation on head `31332d02fc4d63261a16cfbdba7533b808842117`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-cron-notifications.test.ts src/process/exec.test.ts src/cron/command-runner.test.ts src/cron/command-output-summary.test.ts` — passed
  - gateway notification redaction: 6 tests passed across gateway shards
  - cron command summary: 10 tests passed
  - process exec preservation: 16 tests passed
- `git diff --check` — passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/process/exec.ts src/process/exec.test.ts src/cron/command-runner.ts src/cron/command-runner.test.ts src/cron/command-output-summary.ts src/cron/command-output-summary.test.ts src/gateway/server-cron.ts src/gateway/server-cron-notifications.ts src/gateway/server-cron-notifications.test.ts` — passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json ...changed files...` — passed

Regression tests added:

- `src/process/exec.test.ts` verifies preserved lines survive tail truncation.
- `src/cron/command-runner.test.ts` verifies an early `Visit ... enter code ...` line appears in cron command summary even when `outputMaxBytes` keeps only the final tail.
- `src/cron/command-output-summary.test.ts` verifies summary prepending and external redaction.
- `src/gateway/server-cron-notifications.test.ts` verifies command completion webhooks redact URL/code from both `summary` and `diagnostics.summary`.

## Real Behavior Proof

Exact-head proof run: <https://github.com/snowzlmbot/openclaw/actions/runs/28096709237>

Artifact: `cron-action-critical-summary-proof-31332d02fc4d63261a16cfbdba7533b808842117`

The proof checks out exact PR head `31332d02fc4d63261a16cfbdba7533b808842117`, runs a real cron command job with an early device-login style line plus noisy tail output, and verifies both operator summary preservation and external-delivery redaction.

Proof summary:

```text
targetSha=31332d02fc4d63261a16cfbdba7533b808842117
status=ok
summaryContainsActionLine=true
redactedContainsRawUrl=false
redactedContainsRawCode=false
PASS: exact PR head SHA is recorded
PASS: cron command exits successfully
PASS: summary preserves early action-required output
PASS: summary keeps bounded captured tail
PASS: diagnostics mark truncated output
PASS: external delivery summary redacts URL
PASS: external delivery summary redacts code
PASS: external delivery summary omits raw URL
PASS: external delivery summary omits raw code
```

The artifact also includes the focused regression test log for:

- `src/gateway/server-cron-notifications.test.ts`
- `src/process/exec.test.ts`
- `src/cron/command-runner.test.ts`
- `src/cron/command-output-summary.test.ts`

## Related Issues / Existing Candidates

I inspected the three requested issues before opening this PR:

- #96346 remains a valid repair target. Existing candidates #96358/#96365 are blocked by review concerns around real proof and delivery-surface safety; this PR addresses those gaps directly.
- #96363 already has a precise open candidate (#95900), and ClawSweeper explicitly says not to queue a new duplicate repair while that candidate exists.
- #96378 is closed and reviewed as already fixed in current main/latest release; no new repair PR should be opened for it.

## Risk

Low-to-moderate. The exec API grows an optional preservation hook, but default behavior is unchanged. The behavior change is scoped to cron command jobs and bounded by a small preserved-line cap. The external-delivery redaction path reduces accidental leakage risk for auth/setup codes while keeping the run log useful for the operator.
